plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Reduce QQQI and QQQ holdings for a more conservative stance.
Trim CRWD to lower single-name risk.
Maintain a significant cash buffer by reducing overall exposure.
Watch DDOG for a potential small, high-quality addition if conditions are ideal.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for capital preservation, targeting around 1.5% per month. It significantly reduces overall risk by cutting leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration. Expect lower volatility but also more modest growth potential.

Exit SPAL due to below stop
Reduce QQQI due to trim flag
Add strong scanner setup for growth
Add strong scanner setup for growth

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to eliminate flagged holdings and reduce leverage.
Trim QQQI as flagged and SMH due to downtrend.
Add META and DDOG from the scanner for growth potential.
Balance broad index with quality tech, keeping leverage small.
Maintain a cash buffer of approximately 10%.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets around 2.5% per month by focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while adding high-quality scanner setups. Expect moderate volatility and growth.

moves.note: Exit SOXL due to sell flag
Exit SPAL due to below stop
Reduce QQQI due to trim flag

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit flagged positions and reallocate capital.
Trim QQQI to reduce exposure to a downtrending income fund.
Buy META, DDOG, and AMAT, concentrating in highest-scoring scanner setups.
Use leverage strategically by focusing on strong long setups.
Maintain a smaller cash buffer, accepting higher risk for growth.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan aims for stretch growth, targeting around 4% per month. It embraces higher risk by concentrating in top scanner setups and utilizing leverage. Be aware of significantly higher drawdown potential and the daily decay of leveraged ETFs; strict stop discipline is crucial.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI allocation for enhanced monthly distributions.
Reduce QQQ and sell SMH to cut growth and high-beta exposure.
Trim CRWD to minimize single-name, high-beta risk.
Prioritize low drawdown and reliable income over growth, maintaining a cash buffer.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% per month by parking cash for yield. It rotates heavily into income/covered-call ETFs and significantly reduces high-beta holdings. While prioritizing distributions and low drawdown, remember that NAV can still fall, and distributions are not guaranteed.
